project.name: orbit-wars
project.description: Hydra-first reinforcement-learning implementation of Orbit Wars with Torch and JAX training paths, experiment presets, evaluation scripts, and tests.

layers.name: Entrypoints and Operations
layers.description: Command-line training, evaluation, gameplay, and operational scripts.

layers.name: Environment and Features
layers.description: Game simulation, observation features, feature registries, and JAX parity logic.

layers.name: Learning and Policy
layers.description: Policy networks, PPO implementations, normalization, curriculum, opponent selection, and checkpoint support.

layers.name: Configuration and Experiments
layers.description: Hydra schema, config helpers, default templates, presets, sweeps, telemetry, replay, and experiment YAML files.

layers.name: Test Suite
layers.description: Automated pytest coverage for environment, config, feature, policy, PPO, and evaluation behavior.

layers.name: Project Support
layers.description: Miscellaneous support files that do not fit the primary architecture layers.

tour.title: Project Overview
tour.description: Start with the README and project metadata to understand the Hydra-first training workflow and dependency surface.
tour.languageLesson: The project uses Python packaging metadata and Hydra configuration to keep experiment commands reproducible.

tour.title: Training Entrypoints
tour.description: Follow the command-line training modules that launch Torch and JAX reinforcement-learning runs.

tour.title: Environment Model
tour.description: Inspect the core game state, environment stepping, and observation feature construction used by the agents.

tour.title: Policy and Optimization
tour.description: Review policy networks, PPO loops, normalization, curriculum, and opponent pool mechanics.

tour.title: Experiment Configuration
tour.description: Connect Hydra config roots, schemas, presets, and default templates to the training workflow.

tour.title: Validation Coverage
tour.description: Use the test suite to see which environment, feature, policy, PPO, normalization, and config behaviors are guarded.
